participants.reasoning: The extracted criteria align with the official description: AMI patients with proximal and/or mid LAD involvement, age within the reported range, and no contradictory key eligibility details, though the extracted text adds extra exclusion/inclusion specifics not conflicting with the official record.
participants.extracted: Adults aged 20-65 years with a first acute ST-elevation myocardial infarction (AMI) who underwent coronary angiography 1-3 weeks after the event and had proximal and/or mid left anterior descending (LAD) artery involvement, Killip class I-III at admission, and left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) 20-50% by MUGA scan. Patients with multivessel coronary artery disease, pulmonary oedema, Killip class IV, advanced renal or hepatic dysfunction, mechanical complications, prior angioplasty, significant circumflex or right coronary artery involvement, LVEF <20% on echocardiography, PCI within 2 hours of AMI, or pregnancy were excluded.

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official intervention in using intracoronary bone marrow-derived cells versus control, with the main difference being that the official record omits procedural details and gives a slightly broader dose range.
interventions.extracted: Patients in the intervention arm received intracoronary autologous bone marrow-derived mononuclear cells (MNCs) in addition to standard post-PCI medical therapy. Bone marrow (about 100-150 mL) was aspirated from the iliac crest, processed immediately, and the prepared MNCs were infused into the infarct-related left anterior descending artery within four hours of harvest, typically 7-21 days after acute myocardial infarction. The predefined target dose was at least 5 × 10^8 MNCs, although some participants received lower doses (≥2 to <5 × 10^8 cells) and were considered trial deviates. All participants in both groups underwent PCI with TIMI 3 flow and received standard of care medical management; the comparator group received no stem cell infusion (standard care alone).
interventions.official:
Intervention arm: BMSC
Type of stem cells: bone marrow‐derived stem cells
Summary of how stem cells were isolated and type and route of delivery: not reported
Dose of stem cells: 5 to 10 x 10^8 stem cells
Timing of stem cell procedure: not reported
Comparator arm: no additional therapy (control)

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes match the official description in overall content, timing, and MUGA assessment, with only more granular examples listed for secondary events.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure a primary outcome of absolute improvement in left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) by at least 5 percentage points at 6 months after intervention, assessed by multigated acquisition (MUGA) scan compared with baseline. Secondary outcomes were all-cause death during follow-up; recurrent events including repeat acute myocardial infarction, cerebral infarction, and need for target vessel revascularization; hospitalizations for chest pain, breathing difficulty, syncope, heart failure, or arrhythmias; and overall safety/adverse events and serious adverse events during the 6-month follow-up period.
outcomes.official:
Primary outcomes: changes in LVEF from baseline to 6 months
Secondary outcomes: mortality, rehospitalisation for chest pain, heart failure or arrhythmias, and safety of the intervention to 6 months
Outcome assessment points: baseline and 6 months
Method (s): multi‐gated acquisition (MUGA) scan
